data.transformedContent:
<current_datetime>2026-05-16T01:14:42.379Z</current_datetime>

I'm migrating this .NET Framework code to .NET 8 and need to ensure all Thread.Abort
usage is addressed. Check this code and provide your Thread.Abort migration guidance.

```csharp
public class SimpleWorker
{
    public void RunBatch(List<WorkItem> items)
    {
        var threads = new List<Thread>();

        foreach (var item in items)
        {
            var t = new Thread(() => Process(item));
            t.Start();
            threads.Add(t);
        }

        foreach (var t in threads)
            t.Join();
    }

    private void Process(WorkItem item)
    {
        // simulate work
        Thread.Sleep(100);
        item.Result = ComputeResult(item.Input);
    }
}
```

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about migrating Thread.Abort usage to modern .NET. However, looking at the code they provided, it doesn't actually use Thread.Abort, Thread.Interrupt, ThreadAbortException, or Thread.ResetAbort. It only uses Thread.Start, Thread.Join, and Thread.Sleep - which all work identically in modern .NET.

Let me check the skill description for thread-abort-migration:
- "DO NOT USE FOR: code that only uses Thread.Join, Thread.Sleep, or Thread.Start without any abort, interrupt, or ThreadAbortException usage — these APIs work identically in modern .NET and need no migration."


So the skill explicitly says not to use it for this code. I should tell the user that their code doesn't actually use Thread.Abort and doesn't need that migration pattern.
